items.title: Raptors face several challenges in bid to maintain momentum in off-season
items.blurb: Raptors face several challenges in bid to maintain momentum in off-season — It was an enjoyable, refreshing season, but keeping the momentum rolling will be a tough needle to thread for the Raptors. Michael Grange looks at the situation for GM Bobby Webster.

items.title: ‘Doing everything with intent’: Walter focused on improving his game
items.blurb: ‘Doing everything with intent’: Walter focused on improving his game — Toronto Raptors guard Ja’Kobe Walter meets with the media at the team’s year-end media availability and discusses his first playoff experience and the things that he can do to further improve his game and his body for NBA basketball.

items.title: ‘Wanted to be out there with the guys’: Quickley on missing post-season

items.blurb: ‘Where the scouting department earns their money’: How do Raptors improve? — The Raptors Show’s Blake Murphy and Matt Bonner delve into the Toronto Raptors’ roster heading into the off-season, the team’s cap situation, and key role players that the team could bring back for next season.

items.title: ‘Don’t ever want to leave’: RJ Barrett affirms commitment to Raptors
items.blurb: ‘Don’t ever want to leave’: RJ Barrett affirms commitment to Raptors — After cementing himself in Toronto Raptors lore with a season-saving shot in the playoffs, hometown hero RJ Barrett made it clear he doesn’t want to go anywhere.

items.title: Barrett on his Raptors future: ‘Want to be here the rest of my career’

items.title: Scottie Barnes, Raptors run out of road in season that exceeded expectations
items.blurb: Scottie Barnes, Raptors run out of road in season that exceeded expectations — The Toronto Raptors season is over, and by harsh NBA standards, there’s no cause to celebrate. But the challenges ahead will be easier to take on because the Raptors learned definitively that Scottie Barnes is a superstar. The word is out, writes Michael Grange.

items.blurb: ‘Blessed to have him’: Ja’Kobe Walter pinch hits for Ingram-less Raptors — Ja’Kobe Walter answered the call, finishing with 24 points, five rebounds, three steals and a block while shooting 7-of-13 from the field and 4-of-9 from three in 43 minutes.
